chief_complaint: A 2-year-old girl presented with paroxysmal torticollis, which worsened over time, accompanied by ataxia, titubation, dystonia, dyskinesias, seizures, and encephalopathy.
history: The girl was born of third-degree consanguinity and presented with symptoms starting around 2 years of age. The symptoms worsened over time, and she also experienced variable ataxia and seizures. Molecular analysis identified a novel homozygous mutation in the adenosine diphosphate-ribosyl serine hydrolase gene.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Brain MRI showed signal abnormalities in the periventricular white matter and bilateral putamina, with restricted diffusion seen in the latter. Molecular analysis identified a novel homozygous mutation in the adenosine diphosphate-ribosyl serine hydrolase gene (chr1: g.36093274G>T; c.980G>T).
correct_diagnosis: Genotypic and radiological expansion of CONDSIAS
differentials: Genotypic and radiological expansion of CONDSIAS
correct_ranking: Genotypic and radiological expansion of CONDSIAS
